  1. Salesforce
  2. Avocado (SF based) - program registration, membership management
  3. CampDoc - camp & preschool paperwork
  4. Storypark - preschool communication
  5. JDallas Camps - camp communication app (custom app?)
  6. UPace - fitness, access management, Kids Club
  7. IClassPro - Lenny Krayzelburg Swim Academy functionality (?? all swimming or just swim classes?)
  8. Eventive - ticketed events (but sounds like programs are getting in here too - mah jongg for example)
  9. Go Fund Me Pro
  10. Odoo - ?? What do they use this for?? https://www.odoo.com/
  11. does not integrate with GFMP - pain point
  12. FMX - rental management, currently onboarding to this??
  13. "Does not take payments for rentals" - how can that be true?? Website says built in payment processing. Maybe just still figuring it out??



Legacy system:

  1. Thinsoft - did not clean data before importing to new systems, lots of bad data and dupe accounts (where? in SF? in other systems?)

items.content:
Foundational Principles

Organize Around Value Streams, Not Functions Your technology team should be structured around the flows of value your organization delivers to members and community, not around technology disciplines. For a JCC, this typically means organizing around:

Member experience (join, engage, retain)

Program delivery (browse, register, participate, follow-up)

Community operations (facility access, communications, events)
items.plainText: Foundational PrinciplesOrganize Around Value Streams, Not Functions Your technology team should be structured around the flows of value your organization delivers to members and community, not around technology disciplines. For a JCC, this typically means organizing around:Member experience (join, engage, retain)Program delivery (browse, register, participate, follow-up)Community operations (facility access, communications, events)

items.content:
How This Maps to Team Topologies

Stream-Aligned Team: Member & Program Technology This is your value stream team - the product trio above. They own the end-to-end experience of members interacting with your technology. They're empowered to make decisions about what gets built, how systems get configured, what vendors to work with.

Size: Start with 2-3 people (Product Manager + Technical Lead minimum), grow to 4-6 as complexity increases.

Platform Team: IT Infrastructure This team provides reliable, secure infrastructure as a service to the organization. They don't make product decisions about member-facing systems - they ensure everything runs smoothly, securely, and compliantly.

Size: 1-3 people depending on organization size.

Enabling Team: External Partners (That's OpenTent!) You bring in consultants for specialized expertise, system implementations, and to boost capacity during major changes. But the enabling team should be making your internal team more capable, not doing their job for them.
items.plainText: How This Maps to Team TopologiesStream-Aligned Team: Member & Program Technology This is your value stream team - the product trio above. They own the end-to-end experience of members interacting with your technology. They're empowered to make decisions about what gets built, how systems get configured, what vendors to work with.Size: Start with 2-3 people (Product Manager + Technical Lead minimum), grow to 4-6 as complexity increases.Platform Team: IT Infrastructure This team provides reliable, secure infrastructure as a service to the organization. They don't make product decisions about member-facing systems - they ensure everything runs smoothly, securely, and compliantly.Size: 1-3 people depending on organization size.Enabling Team: External Partners (That's OpenTent!) You bring in consultants for specialized expertise, system implementations, and to boost capacity during major changes. But the enabling team should be making your internal team more capable, not doing their job for them.

items.content:
Where Teams Sit Organizationally

Critical: The Product Trio sits in OPERATIONS, not IT

This is where most JCCs get it wrong. Here's why:

Member & Program Technology reports to COO/VP of Operations because:

They need to be close to the business problems, not the technical solutions

They make daily trade-off decisions that impact program delivery, member experience, and revenue

They need authority to say "no" to requests that don't serve member outcomes

They need to understand operations rhythms (program seasons, membership cycles, camp registration)

IT Infrastructure reports to CFO or COO because:

They're managing risk, compliance, and organizational capabilities

They operate on different timeframes (infrastructure planning vs product iteration)

Their success metrics are different (uptime, security, cost) vs (adoption, satisfaction, business outcomes)
items.plainText: Where Teams Sit OrganizationallyCritical: The Product Trio sits in OPERATIONS, not ITThis is where most JCCs get it wrong. Here's why:Member & Program Technology reports to COO/VP of Operations because:They need to be close to the business problems, not the technical solutionsThey make daily trade-off decisions that impact program delivery, member experience, and revenueThey need authority to say "no" to requests that don't serve member outcomesThey need to understand operations rhythms (program seasons, membership cycles, camp registration)IT Infrastructure reports to CFO or COO because:They're managing risk, compliance, and organizational capabilitiesThey operate on different timeframes (infrastructure planning vs product iteration)Their success metrics are different (uptime, security, cost) vs (adoption, satisfaction, business outcomes)

items.content:
1. A lot of manual follow up on things like renewal notices, bad debt
  2. Hard to follow up on new memberships without automation
  3. A lot of clicks to purchase a membership
  4. Hard to add additional members to account
  5. Not a mobile friendly interface for Avocado
  6. Members have to use a lot of different applications
  7. Guest pass is a pain, adds guest to member account
  8. Hard to discern all the ways a member is using the facility, follow and automate communication about the member journey (30/60/90 touchpoints)
  9. Senior Social Memberships do not give member pricing for eligible programs
  10. People cannot pay for something on someone else’s account without putting
    a CC on that account
